commit.message:
feat(forge): add Design-First mode with progressive design levels

Implement Design-First collaboration pattern (inspired by Martin Fowler's
article) as a formal DESIGN phase in the Forge agent pipeline.

New DESIGN mode (modes/design.md):
- 4 progressive design levels: Capabilities → Components → Interactions → Contracts
- Each level is a checkpoint requiring user approval before advancing
- Quality Constraints (NFRs) in L1 — architecture-shaping constraints
- Domain Model + Trust Boundaries in L2 — DDD-informed aggregate boundaries
- Failure Modes (required) + State Machines + Event Topology in L3
- Event Contracts + Error Types + Migration Notes in L4
- Complexity-adaptive entry points: T2 contracts-only, T3 components+, T4-T5 full
- Conditional sections activated by triggers, not mandatory ritual

Enhanced existing modes:
- IDEATE: design questions per approach for collaborative refinement
- EXECUTE: Contract-Driven TDD protocol for T3+ tasks with frozen contracts
- VERIFY: Scope Drift Audit + Contract Conformance checks

Updated routing and contracts:
- SKILL.md: DESIGN added to intent classification between IDEATE and PLAN
- MODE_CONTRACTS.md: DESIGN contract + updated transition rules
- ROUTING_RULES.md: design-depth calibration table + routing decision tree
- Build scripts: all 3 platforms (sh, ps1, gpt-ps1) include design mode

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add comprehensive live event streaming design (Hotstar/Twitch/Netflix)

846-line design document focused on live sports/concert streaming:

Architecture: Camera → Ingest PoP (RTMP/SRT) → GPU Transcoder →
Packager (HLS/DASH/CMAF) → Origin → Origin Shield → Multi-CDN → Viewers

Key sections:
- Capacity estimation: 50M concurrent, 150 Tbps CDN bandwidth,
  25M segment requests/sec, 417K chat messages/sec
- Ingest pipeline: RTMP/SRT, dual-ingest redundancy
- Live transcoding: GPU-accelerated, 5-rendition bitrate ladder
  (240p-1080p), 2s segment duration trade-off analysis
- Packaging: HLS/DASH/CMAF comparison, DRM (Widevine/FairPlay/PlayReady)
- CDN delivery: origin shield, request coalescing for thundering
  herd, multi-CDN strategy, push-based cache population
- ABR playback: throughput vs buffer-based algorithms, player state machine
- DVR seek-back: ring buffer, 4h window, post-event VOD reuse
- Low-latency: comparison table (standard 15-30s → LL-HLS 2-4s →
  CMAF-LL 2-3s → WebRTC <1s)
- Live chat: WebSocket, Redis pub/sub, message sampling at scale
- Analytics: Kafka→Flink pipeline, QoE metrics, 38M events/sec
- Highlights/clipping: near-instant from DVR segments
- Pre-event scaling: proactive scale-up, Game Day chaos testing,
  circuit breakers, cache warming

10 Mermaid diagrams (architecture, ingest, transcoding, packaging,
CDN hierarchy, thundering herd, ABR state machine, chat, analytics,
clipping)

Real-world notes: Disney+ Hotstar (50M+ IPL), Twitch (~100 PoPs),
Netflix Live (Open Connect 18K servers, WWE Raw), YouTube Live

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add retry with exponential backoff for API requests

Configure the github3.py session with urllib3 Retry (5 retries,
exponential backoff: 1s/2s/4s/8s/16s) and increase the default
read timeout from 10s to 30s. This prevents transient timeouts
when paginating large repos like github/github with 1400+ PRs.

Retries on: 429 (rate limit), 500, 502, 503, 504.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Auto-create data pipeline flows via Flow Management API

register-form now automatically creates a PA data pipeline flow
after registration using the Flow Management REST API:

POST https://api.flow.microsoft.com/.../environments/{env}/flows

- New shared/flow_api_client.py handles token + API call
- register_form/handler.py calls generate_flow_definition()
  internally then deploys via flow_api_client
- Graceful fallback: if flow creation fails, registration
  still succeeds and logs a warning
- POWER_PLATFORM_ENVIRONMENT_ID env var (auto-discovered if unset)
- No Premium connector needed — uses standard HTTP + managed identity

Zero clinician effort: submit registration form → both registry
entry AND data pipeline flow are created automatically.

commit.message:
fix: ensure tileColors present and fix DPR canvas scaling

Two issues causing the blue-screen-with-labels-only rendering:

1. Missing tileColors: Layouts loaded from cache or disk may omit
   tileColors entirely. The renderer's renderTileGrid skips all tiles
   when tileColors is undefined/empty — resulting in no floor or wall
   tiles drawn. Added ensureTileColors() that generates colors from
   the tiles array using default floor/wall colors when missing.

2. DPR scaling: Canvas was created at devicePixelRatio size but the
   2D context never applied ctx.scale(dpr, dpr). All canvas rendering
   was in physical pixels, not CSS pixels, causing content to appear
   at wrong size and position on high-DPI displays. Labels (HTML)
   rendered at CSS coordinates while canvas content was misaligned.
   Added ctx.setTransform(dpr, ...) at the start of each frame.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(LegalLinks): expose crawlable Privacy and Terms links for OAuth branding verification (#217) Google OAuth branding verification flagged `https://wingdex.app` because the homepage did not expose a detectable link to the Privacy Policy. The legal links were rendered as button-driven SPA navigation instead of real anchors, and the same pattern also affected the Terms-to-Privacy cross-link. - **What changed** - Replaced footer `Privacy` and `Terms` controls with real anchor links: - `/#privacy` - `/#terms` - Preserved in-app navigation by letting the browser follow those anchors and updating the hash router on `hashchange`. - Updated the Terms page's `Privacy Policy` reference to use the same real URL instead of a non-resolving fragment. - Kept standard link behavior intact so modified clicks like Cmd/Ctrl-click still work normally. - **Router behavior** - Added `hashchange` handling so anchor-based legal navigation updates the rendered page even when navigation comes from plain `href` changes rather than tab clicks. - **Why this matters** - The homepage now contains explicit legal page URLs that crawlers and OAuth branding review can detect. - Privacy and Terms remain navigable both as direct URLs and through normal SPA interactions. - The footer links now retain native browser semantics for opening in a new tab or window. ```tsx Privacy ``` - **Screenshot** - OAuth verification issue example: `https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d7be9e8e-27bf-4b54-a344-27dc3a111541` > [!WARNING] > > --- ✨ Let Copilot coding agent [set things up for you](https://github.com/jlian/wingdex/issues/new?title=✨+Set+up+Copilot+instructions&body=Configure%20instructions%20for%20this%20repository%20as%20documented%20in%20%5BBest%20practices%20for%20Copilot%20coding%20agent%20in%20your%20repository%5D%28https://gh.io/copilot-coding-agent-tips%29%2E%0A%0A%3COnboard%20this%20repo%3E&assignees=copilot) — coding agent works faster and does higher quality work when set up for your repo. --------- Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> Co-authored-by: jlian <2320572+jlian@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: use aggregate_current for SIMILAR total type in percentile calc (#6750)

* cleanup: remove unreachable defensive error logs in aggregate graph item validation

* fix: use aggregate_current for SIMILAR total type in percentile calc

When computing Nth percentile for aggregate graphs with "Total Similar
Data Sources" total type, the code incorrectly used aggregate_sum (which
sums all data sources together) instead of aggregate_current (which
filters by matching data_source_name). This caused in/out 95th
percentile HRULEs to display identical values on aggregate graphs.

Split the SIMILAR and ALL total type cases in three locations:
- aggregate_graphs_insert_graph_items() text_format substitution
- aggregate_handle_ptile_type() for COMMENT items
- aggregate_handle_ptile_type() for HRULE items

SIMILAR now uses aggregate_current / aggregate_current_peak, which
compute percentiles per data source name across member graphs. ALL
continues to use aggregate_sum / aggregate_sum_peak.

Fixes #6470

Signed-off-by: Thomas Vincent <thomasvincent@gmail.com>

* test: verify SIMILAR total type uses per-data-source percentile calculation

AGGREGATE_TOTAL_TYPE_SIMILAR was grouped with ALL in three locations in
lib/api_aggregate.php, causing SIMILAR graphs to use aggregate_sum /
aggregate_sum_peak (summed totals) instead of aggregate_current /
aggregate_current_peak (per-source percentiles). Split each if block
into separate ALL and SIMILAR elseif branches.

lib/api_aggregate.php is the intentional target of this fix. This is not
cleanup or drift from another branch -- the three buggy if conditions
were always here and are the root cause of issue #6470.

Fixes #6470

Add convex hull vs halfspace narrowphase collision

Implement phys_convex_hull_vs_halfspace() for detecting collisions between
convex hull shapes and infinite halfspace planes (ground/walls). Transforms
hull vertices to world space, finds deepest penetrating vertices, and returns
up to 4 contacts with correct normals.

Add dispatch for CONVEX × HALFSPACE in all three narrowphase paths:
- narrowphase.c (serial)
- narrowphase_par.c (parallel)
- collision_fused_par.c (fused pipeline)

Fix two solver issues that prevented effective ground collision response:
- Set default baumgarte to 0.2 (was 0.0) so contacts provide position
  correction velocity, not just velocity-level prevention
- Cap baumgarte bias at 10 m/s to prevent explosive impulses from deep
  penetration (common when bodies fall through before detection)

Include ghost bodies in island merging so skeleton joints and ground contacts
end up in the same solver island. Without this, joint pseudo-velocity
corrections could fight ground contact corrections across separate islands.

Remove temporary debug output from tick_parallel.c, integrate_par.c,
and collision_fused_par.c.

* fix(plugin-axum): align tsconfig with monorepo composite build pattern

* fix(testing): use CI PostgreSQL service instead of embedded-postgres in e2e tests

embedded-postgres fails on CI runners with exit code 127 (initdb not found).
When DATABASE_URL is set (CI), create isolated databases on the provided
PostgreSQL service. Fall back to embedded-postgres for local development.

Each test creates its own database (typokit_e2e_server, typokit_e2e_axum)
to avoid conflicts when Nx runs them in parallel.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(docs): align example-todo-server-axum tsconfig with monorepo pattern

The Nx typescript-sync added example-todo-server-axum to the root
tsconfig.json references because the package was missing tsconfig.lib.json.
TypeDoc (via starlight-typedoc) then discovered this package through
the root tsconfig reference and copied its README into _media/, causing
Astro's content collection to fail (missing title frontmatter).

Fix:
- Add tsconfig.lib.json and tsconfig.spec.json following the monorepo pattern
- Update tsconfig.json to use the standard references structure
- Revert root tsconfig.json references back to empty (Nx sync confirms clean)

* fix(plugin-axum): use absolute URLs in README to prevent TypeDoc media copying

TypeDoc resolves relative links in package READMEs and copies the target
directories into _media/. The relative link to example-todo-server-axum
caused a 2GB+ _media copy that broke the Astro content loader (missing
title frontmatter). Replace relative links with absolute GitHub URLs.

PostgreSQL service. Add rstest.config.ts to example-todo-server that
excludes e2e.test.ts from the default test command. E2E tests continue
to run via the separate 'test:e2e' target in the dedicated e2e CI job.
